preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.21.1: P0 HOTFIX — restore mcp__merlin__* tools. v1.21.0 bumped @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk to 0.2.123, which has a regression in its session-resume path: passing `resume:` to query() causes in-process MCP servers (registered via createSdkMcpServer) to silently disappear from Claude's tool list. The SDK's init message still reports `merlin: connected` but the actual tool definitions never reach the spawned subprocess. Every paying user on v1.21.0 hit this on their second message in any brand because per-brand sessionId persistence in .merlin-threads.json triggers resume on every turn. Repro: identical setup, only difference is `resume:` flag → fresh session sees all 16+ mcp__merlin__* tools, resumed session sees zero. Fix: skip the `queryOptions.resume = resumeSessionId` assignment in main.js until Anthropic ships an SDK fix. Trade-off: each user turn becomes a fresh SDK session — Claude no longer carries SDK-level conversation memory between turns. Per-turn context still reaches Claude via (a) the bubble history rendered by the renderer + sent on resume, (b) the system-prompt brand-active hint, (c) the auto-memory layer, (d) the prior conversation file at ~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/<sessionId>.jsonl which the SDK still references for tool-call history. Users will notice Claude doesn't remember context across turns at the SDK level — but tools work, which is the whole point. Restore path documented in the regression-guard comment at app/main.js:3102; flip back to the original branch the moment the SDK fix lands. New regression test: app/main-resume-mcp-loss.test.js asserts the resume assignment stays disabled (source-scan guard preventing accidental restoration). Companion fix to v1.21.0's silent /update drift: this is also the first release that benefits from PR #173's hard-fail Phase 1 + self-heal. Existing v1.21.0 users will receive v1.21.1 cleanly via /update unless their Phase 1 fetch silently failed before — those users will need to reinstall once via fresh MerlinSetup.exe to leapfrog. | v1.21.0: Major release bundling FOUR independent improvements. (1) Google Analytics 4 read+write MCP tool surface — six READ actions (discover, traffic, conversions, attribution, landing-pages, audit-property) and seven WRITE actions (create-key-event, archive-key-event, create-custom-dimension, create-custom-metric, create-audience, update-property-settings, attach-shopify-events). All writes gated by cmd.Approved (the Electron approval-card flag the renderer flips on user click) — the binary refuses to call any Admin API write endpoint without it, mirroring the Reddit organic-post safety rail. attach-shopify-events is the high-value one: idempotent one-click setup that marks the standard ecommerce funnel events (purchase, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, view_item, view_item_list, search, sign_up, generate_lead) as GA4 key events, replacing 30 minutes of click-through-Admin with one tool call. The OAuth scope bundle now carries analytics.edit (strict superset of analytics.readonly — covers Data API runReport AND Admin API writes); existing connected users re-consent ONCE on next Google action since Google's OAuth handles scope upgrades natively. landing-pages action populates the seo-signals.json GA4 slot end-to-end so SEO clusters can finally score URLs against real engagement. Architecturally split: analytics.go ships reads (only GETs + the Data-API-read-only POST runReport); analytics_writes.go ships every mutation through analyticsAdminMutate (the only POST/PATCH/DELETE constructor) with requireApproval(action, cmd) as the first statement. Every Data + Admin API call routes through PreflightCheck + RecordSuccess/RecordRateLimitHit per Hard-Won Security Rule 4. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 18 documents the surface, the four required call-sites for adding a write runner, and the regression guards. New Go tests: TestAnalyticsWriteActionsRequireApprovalFlag (per-runner refuse-without-approval), TestEveryWriteRunnerCallsRequireApproval (static grep guard), TestAttachShopifyEventsIsIdempotent (8 POSTs first run, 0 second), TestAttachShopifyEventsRefusesWithoutShopifyConnection, plus the standard preflight / wire shape / regression-guard tests for the 6 reads. (2) Meta Audit MCP tool — read-only inspection layer for Meta ad assets. Seven new actions on the new mcp__merlin__meta_audit tool, all GETs against the Graph API (no writes, no spend, safe to call freely): list-audiences (every custom audience, sorted newest-first, with operation/delivery status + approximate count), audit-audience-rule (full targeting rule for one audience — pass adId), audit-retargeting-cascade (walks active ad sets, cross-references custom_audiences ∩ excluded_custom_audiences, flags the classic 'include site visitors, forget to exclude purchasers' leak — the #1 cause of paying to retarget your own buyers), list-conversions (every custom conversion: id/name/event_type/last_fired/archived/default_value), audit-pixel (pixel diagnostics: match_rate_approx, server_events_match_rate, last_fired_time, automatic-matching state, top events over last 7d, plus per-pixel findings: low match rate, no recent fires, missing Purchase events, automatic matching off), audit-frequency-caps (every active ad set's frequency_control_specs; flags ad sets with no cap as fatigue risk), audit-catalog (review_status counts + top disapproval reasons + sample of disapproved products — pass catalogId from meta_ads({action:'catalog'})). Architecturally read-only by construction: the entire meta_audit.go file uses only metaGet (GET-only by design — Hard-Won Security Rule 4), every action goes through PreflightCheck + RecordSuccess/RecordRateLimitHit so audit fan-outs (e.g. cascade walks 200 ad sets) cannot stampede past Meta's rate-limit budget. The MCP tool registration sets destructive:false + preview:false + costImpact:'api' — the agent can call any audit action without a confirmation card or budget validation. Use cases that previously required a trip to Ads Manager: 'audit my retargeting' (catches buyer-retargeting leaks), 'is my pixel healthy' (low match rate often means missing em/ph/fbp/fbc params, CAPI typically lifts to 70%+), 'are my ad sets capped' (no cap → fatigue risk grows with spend; recommends 1 imp/7d for prospecting, 3 imp/7d for retargeting), 'is my catalog healthy' (Meta will skip OOS/disapproved products in DPA — scaling spend on a broken catalog amplifies impression waste). merlin-ads SKILL gets a new Meta Audit table + 7 routing-hint patterns covering the natural-language utterances ('audit my retargeting', 'list my custom audiences', 'what's my pixel match quality', etc.). 25 new Go tests in meta_audit_test.go (token-required preflight × 5, retargeting cascade findings × 4, name-pattern recognizers × 2, pixel diagnostic findings × 6, HTTP wire shape × 7, regression-guard structural test × 1) + new MCP-tools test that meta_audit must NOT carry the destructive annotation (a future copy-paste regression that flips it to destructive would silently turn on preview gating for inspection actions). Total Go tests green. Estimated impact: dashboard-grade ad account hygiene checks become a one-tool-call away — most DTC accounts have at least one live cascade leak the audit catches inside 30 seconds. (3) Mobile WebSocket keepalive — fixes the 'connection fails after 5 min even while I'm using it on my phone' incident (live, 2026-04-30 POG mobile). Root cause was zero application-level WebSocket keepalive: browser WebSocket spec doesn't expose ping/pong control to JS, the `ws` Node library has no default ping (the pre-fix comment in relay-client.js claiming 'the runtime sends WS pings automatically' was wrong), and Cloudflare's hibernatable WebSockets don't auto-PING either. Mobile carrier NAT idle timeouts (1-5 min typical) silently drop idle WS TCP connections, which look alive on every layer until first send-attempt. Fix lands as a coordinated four-side change: relay/durable.js exact-matches `{type:"ping"}` BEFORE rate-limit accounting and replies `{type:"pong"}` (so pings cost zero rate-limit budget and never get forwarded — each leg keeps its own NAT alive independently); app/relay-client.js and pwa/pwa.js send `{type:"ping"}` every 25s (below the 30s floor of every common NAT idle timeout) and force-close any leg that hasn't seen ANY inbound frame within 60s (so the OS's much-slower dead-connection detection doesn't strand the user); app/ws-server.js handles the ping BEFORE the auth gate so a paired phone re-foregrounding a backgrounded tab keeps its NAT alive even during the auth-replay window. Source-pinned regression tests: relay's worker.test.mjs asserts the ping check precedes msgWindowCount++ accounting (otherwise idle clients eat the budget at scale); ws-server.test.js asserts the ping check precedes the `if (!authed)` gate; relay-client.test.js asserts PING_INTERVAL_MS < 30s (NAT floor) and PONG_DEADLINE_MS >= 2x PING_INTERVAL_MS (single-loss tolerance); both sides assert the literal compact JSON frame string '{"type":"ping"}' so a future stringify(...) refactor can't burn the rate-limit budget. (4) Mac DMG ffmpeg fallback — release.yml now tries evermeet.cx first, falls back to deolaha.ca on connection failure (both serve a SHA-identical artifact since deolaha.ca IS evermeet.cx's storage; the redirect is the user-facing convenience). Same SHA verification runs on either source. Pinned --connect-timeout 30 --max-time 300 so a hanging endpoint short-circuits to the fallback in <60s rather than blocking on curl's default DNS-resolve timeout. Live incident anchor: the v1.21.0 Mac DMG build failed twice (curl exit 7) on 2026-04-30 because evermeet.cx was unreachable from GitHub Actions Mac runners for ~50 minutes; the deolaha.ca mirror was up the entire time. | v1.20.8: HOTFIX — Klaviyo flow-create body shape conforms to the OpenAPI spec at revision 2026-04-15. v1.20.7 inferred the shape from prose in Klaviyo's docs and got it materially wrong; live import returned 'No valid revisions found for method' on POST /api/flows. The actual schema (FlowCreateQuery → FlowCreateQueryResourceObject → FlowDefinition → {ListTrigger,SegmentTrigger,MetricTrigger,ProfilePropertyDateTrigger,SendEmailAction → SendEmailActionData → FlowEmail, TimeDelayAction, Link}) requires five things v1.20.7 missed: (1) trigger.type enum is `list` / `segment` / `metric` / `date` — NOT `list_added` / `ecommerce_*` / `custom_event`; the manifest's friendly names now translate via triggerTypeToKlaviyoEnum. (2) every action carries a deterministic `temporary_id` (a0, a1, a2, ...) — entry_action_id points to a0; previously actions had no IDs and Klaviyo had no way to wire the graph. (3) actions are linked via the `links` field — non-terminal `{next: "aN+1"}`, terminal `{next: null}` (the field is required + nullable per spec Link); v1.20.7 omitted links entirely so the action sequence wasn't a valid graph. (4) `entry_action_id` is required on FlowDefinition; v1.20.7 omitted it. (5) attributes now contains ONLY {name, definition} — no `status`, no `trigger_type` (both forbidden by FlowCreateQueryResourceObject); status=manual|live now fires a follow-up PATCH /api/flows/{id} after create succeeds. Send-email action body shape rewritten: SendEmailActionData wraps a FlowEmail in `data.message` (NOT directly in data); FlowEmail uses `subject_line` not `subject`, `from_label` not `from_name`. Time-delay shape rewritten: TimeDelayActionData is `{unit, value}` — NOT `{delay_seconds}` (no such field exists in the spec); translateDelaySeconds picks the largest exact unit (3600s → hours/1, 86400s → days/1) for clean Klaviyo UI rendering. Metric-backed triggers (ecommerce_*, custom_event, viewed_product, back_in_stock, profile_subscribed_marketing) now REQUIRE a `metric_id` on the manifest — Klaviyo identifies metrics by ULID, not name; pull from klaviyo({action:'performance'}) and put the ULID in trigger.metric_id. New klaviyoFlowsRequest helper sends revision header `2026-04-15` (the rest of the binary's Klaviyo surface stays on 2024-10-15 — bumping globally would risk silent breakage on lists/segments/profiles). Bulk-import path zeros flow.Status before passing to klaviyoCreateFlowFull to avoid double-PATCH (the create function now also fires the status flip when called directly). 8 new schema-conformance tests in klaviyo_flows_test.go (TestBuildFlowCreatePayload_*: HappyPath_LinearWelcomeSeries, TriggerTypeMapping × 10 sub-tests, MetricTriggerRequiresMetricID, ListTriggerRequiresListID, UnknownTriggerTypeRefused, StatusFlipFiresAfterCreate, SchemaConformsToOpenAPI, SendEmailUsesSubjectLineNotSubject, TerminalActionLinksNextIsNull, TestTranslateDelaySeconds × 8 cases) plus the existing 26 — 35 total Go flow tests, all green. Live import now succeeds against revision 2026-04-15. v1.20.7: Klaviyo Flows are now fully programmatic (correction). The v1.20.1 release notes + the merlin-social SKILL incorrectly claimed Klaviyo Flows were UI-only — that was wrong. Klaviyo's public API exposes the full flow surface (list, create with inline definition blob, update status, delete, plus optional step-level CRUD) at revision 2024-10-15. v1.20.7 closes the gap with klaviyo({action: 'flows-bulk-import', brand, manifestPath}) — drop a JSON manifest of email automations under assets/brands/<brand>/email/flows.json and Merlin creates them all via the Flows API. Same shape as the v1.20.2 Postscript bulk-flow-import; flows reference templates by their template_id from a prior templates-bulk-upload pass. Six new actions: flows-list / flow-get / flow-create / flow-update-status / flow-delete / flows-bulk-import. CAN-SPAM gate (the email equivalent of Postscript's TCPA gate) refuses any flow whose trigger.type is not on the documented-consent allowlist (list_added, segment_added, profile_subscribed_marketing, ecommerce_placed_order, ecommerce_started_checkout, viewed_product, custom_event), or whose send_email steps are missing an unsubscribe token / physical mailing address (inline OR resolvable from brand.md Address:) / subject / from_name. Failures surface verbatim — never auto-fixed; Ryan sees exactly which flow blocked and why. Sequential POST with 6s spacing — flows share klaviyoBulkUploadConcurrency=1 + klaviyoBulkUploadMinSpacingMs=6000 with templates because Klaviyo's per-minute cap is plan-tier-global, not per-endpoint. Live-state dedup: bulk-import lists existing flows once and refuses to create a duplicate-by-name (idempotent re-imports); pass forceReimport=true to override. The merlin-social SKILL was corrected in the same commit — the prior 'flows are UI-only' wording is replaced with the correct programmatic surface + manifest schema. New tests: 26 Go tests in klaviyo_flows_test.go (list pagination, get with definition include, create body shape, step ordering preserved, send_email/delay action mapping, status PATCH, delete, CAN-SPAM gate × 9 rules, bulk-import happy path, gate-failure-blocks-only-that-flow, duplicate-skipped-vs-forceReimport, manifest status=live triggers PATCH, sequential pacing, manifest path traversal × 3); 4 new Node tests in mcp-tools.test.js (description references Flows + bulk-import + CAN-SPAM, no UI-only claim, flows-list dispatches, flow-create accepts flowBody, flows-bulk-import reaches engine layer with brand). v1.20.6: HOTFIX — MCP shim resilience + diagnostic logging + version-fallback fix. Three small targeted fixes in app/merlin-mcp-shim.js after Ryan's POG /update incident at 2026-04-29 17:17:30 produced 'Server transport closed unexpectedly' in Claude Desktop with NO stderr to diagnose. (1) Process-lifetime guard. Pre-fix, when the IPC socket dropped (e.g. Merlin desktop app restart during /update), the shim's destroy() handler cleared all pending requests + nulled the socket. With no MCP message in flight, Node's event loop ran out of active handles and the shim exited 0 silently. Claude Desktop saw 'process exiting early' and reported Server disconnected with no actionable info. Fix: explicit process.stdin.resume() at startup so stdin's read handle keeps the event loop alive between MCP messages, plus a 30s setInterval(...).unref() heartbeat as belt-and-suspenders. The shim now ONLY exits when its parent (Claude Desktop) closes stdin or kills it — never because it ran out of background work. (2) Stderr diagnostic logging. Per Claude Desktop's own log footer ('If you are developing this MCP server you can add output to stderr...'), every error path now writes a labeled line to stderr (NOT stdout — stdout is the MCP framing channel). Covers: startup banner with pid + stateDir + version, stdin parse errors, dispatch errors, IPC connect failures, IPC socket close (informational, expected during desktop-app restart), IPC socket errors, tools/list IPC failure paths, uncaughtException + unhandledRejection. Future 'Server disconnected' events come with a cause inline. (3) readVersion fallback fix. Pre-fix the shim ships at resources/app.asar.unpacked/app/merlin-mcp-shim.js but readVersion() only walked one level up to look for package.json — landing at resources/app.asar.unpacked/package.json which doesn't exist. The version came back as 'unknown' for every initialize response (visible in Claude Desktop logs). Now tries three candidate paths to handle the packaged-asar layout AND the dev-tree layout. SERVER_VERSION exported for test assertions. New tests in app/merlin-mcp-shim.test.js: readVersion returns a real semver (no more 'unknown' silent regression), SERVER_VERSION constant resolves at module load, and a regression test that proves createIpcClient is reusable after destroy() (next .send() reconnects cleanly — pre-fix this would either throw or hang because destroy() poisoned the client). 30 total shim tests, all green. v1.20.5: HOTFIX — 'Add to Claude Code' now actually works. Live incident (2026-04-29 morning, Ryan / pog-shopify): v1.20.0's Claude Desktop autoconfig prompt fired in Ryan's running Claude Code session, he clicked 'Add', and the merlin entry landed in <project>/.mcp.json — but Claude Code refused to load it because pog-shopify's projects[<path>].enabledMcpjsonServers in ~/.claude.json was an empty array. The two clients use DIFFERENT config models: Claude Desktop is a single ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json (or %APPDATA%\Claude on Windows) where any mcpServers entry auto-loads; Claude Code is a two-file split where <project>/.mcp.json carries the registration but ~/.claude.json's per-project enabledMcpjsonServers allowlist gates loading (security: a teammate checking in .mcp.json cannot silently inject a server). Fix lands as a strict superset of v1.20.0's behavior — the Desktop flow is unchanged. New helpers in app/claude-desktop-config.js: claudeCodeConfigPath, claudeCodeProjectMcpPath, detectInstalledClients, resolveProjectKey (rejects empties + non-dirs, fs.realpathSync to collapse symlinks), mergeMcpJsonMerlinEntry (per-project, same shape as Desktop merge), mergeClaudeJsonEnable (touches EXACTLY projects[<key>].enabledMcpjsonServers + auto-prunes from disabledMcpjsonServers if present, never invents the disabled field, never touches any other ~/.claude.json field), isRegisteredClaudeCode (true iff BOTH files match), listClaudeCodeProjectsStatus (per-project live/stale dot for the sidecar), applyRegistrationClaudeCode (TWO atomic tmp+rename writes, refuses corrupt JSON on either side, idempotent on re-run, remembers project in <stateDir>/.mcp-claude-code-projects.json). Autoprompt now detects which client(s) are installed (cheap statSync of Claude Desktop's config dir + readFileSync of ~/.claude.json) and tailors buttons accordingly — Desktop-only / Code-only / both-detected (Add Desktop / Add Code / Add Both / Skip / Don't ask). The Claude Code branch opens an Electron showOpenDialog directory picker (defaults to most-recently-registered project, then ~/Documents, then homedir; user picks the project folder). New IPC handlers: mcp-autoconfig-add-claude-code (renderer-driven equivalent of the autoprompt's Code branch) + mcp-autoconfig-status returns extended shape { desktop: {configured, configPath}, code: {configured, claudeJsonPath, projects: [{path, enabled}]}, socketAlive, shimPath, registered (alias) }; v1.20.0 callers still see the legacy `registered` + `configPath` aliases. Safety rails: atomic writes ONLY (tmp + rename, mode 0o600 on POSIX), refuse-to-clobber on corrupt JSON for both files (clear error pointing the user at the path to repair manually), realpath project-key resolution to prevent double-registering same project under two key names via symlink, never touch any ~/.claude.json field outside projects[<key>].enabledMcpjsonServers / .disabledMcpjsonServers (the file holds the user's settings, theme, history pointer, onboarding state — clobbering it would wipe Claude Code state). Tests: 26 new in app/claude-desktop-config.test.js (45 total, was 19) — covers every described behavior + edge cases (no projects map, project entry without MCP arrays, append without removing siblings, idempotent re-run, disabled-list pruning, top-level field preservation, other-project preservation, no-disabled-field invention, corrupt .mcp.json refusal, corrupt ~/.claude.json refusal, missing-dir validation, both-files-required for isRegisteredClaudeCode true, dedup+sort+atomic for project memory, detectInstalledClients shape contract). Manual fixture-diff verification across 7 realistic shapes (fresh Claude Code, project-with-other-settings, the exact pog-shopify live-incident shape, merlin-was-disabled, idempotent re-run, .mcp.json with sibling, stale merlin entry overwrite) confirmed expected diffs in dev. v1.20.4: HOTFIX — Merlin works in Claude Code now. Live incident (2026-04-29 morning, after the v1.20.0 sidecar shipped): the stdio MCP shim handshake succeeded with Claude Code (272ms, 45 tools advertised) but Claude Code rejected EVERY tool because each tool's inputSchema was missing a top-level `type: "object"`. Per the MCP spec + JSON Schema 7, every tool's inputSchema MUST be a JSON Schema object with `type: "object"` at the top level. Claude Desktop is permissive and silently accepts schemas without this; Claude Code is strict and refuses every non-conformant tool. Root cause: the IPC endpoint's toJsonSchema() in app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.js called `zod-to-json-schema` (designed for zod 3) on the SDK's zod 4 schemas. zod-to-json-schema given a zod 4 ZodObject returns `{$schema: '...'}` ONLY — no type, no properties, no required — which after the existing `$schema` strip became `{}` for every tool. Two-part fix: (1) prefer zod 4's native `z.toJSONSchema(obj, {unrepresentable: 'any'})` when available, fall back to zod-to-json-schema only if a future zod 3 downgrade ever happens; (2) REGRESSION GUARD that forces `type: 'object'` + presence of `properties` + explicit `additionalProperties` on the result regardless of converter output, so drift in any future converter / zod version cannot reach the wire. Smoke test against the live tool registry: 45 tools, 0 with non-conformant inputSchema. Before/after on the empty-zod-object worst case: BEFORE shipped `{}`, AFTER ships `{type:"object", properties:{}, additionalProperties:false}`. New tests: 8 toJsonSchema/buildToolsListPayload tests covering empty input, empty zod, normal zod, enum + nested objects, presence invariants, required-field preservation, and an anti-regression that asserts every tool in a mixed pathological registry has type:"object" before reaching the wire. v1.20.3: HOTFIX — Klaviyo bulk-template-upload pacing. Live incident on the POG 27-flow import: ~10 templates landed cleanly, then 8 of the next batch returned 429 from Klaviyo, marking the files as permanently failed and requiring a manual re-run. Three stacked causes: (1) klaviyoBulkUploadConcurrency was 3, three goroutines hit PreflightCheck within the same nanosecond, all observed '0 calls in last minute' before any RecordSuccess incremented — thundering herd; (2) Klaviyo's POST /api/templates/ per-minute cap is plan-dependent, free/email tiers can be ~10/min vs the documented 150/min steady the ratelimit_preflight default assumed; (3) klaviyoCreateTemplate surfaced 429 verbatim with no retry. Fix: concurrency 3 → 1 (sequential, eliminates herd race), 6s minimum spacing between calls (10/min ceiling), klaviyoCreateTemplateWithRetry wraps with a single 35s-backoff retry on 429. Trade-off: 27-template import goes from ~9s to ~3min — acceptable for a one-time setup. lastCallAt updates on failure too so a 429 doesn't fire the next call into a hot Klaviyo window. New tests: TestIsKlaviyoRateLimitError pins the 429 substring contract, TestKlaviyoCreateTemplateWithRetry_NoRetryOnSuccess verifies happy path skips the retry sleep. v1.20.2: Postscript SMS full-coverage automation API + bulk-flow import. Postscript exposes Automations (their flow analog) as a fully-scriptable public API, unlike Klaviyo Flows which are dashboard-only — so the morning POG SMS setup can land as a single mcp__merlin__postscript({action: 'bulk-import-flow', manifestPath, brand}) call where the equivalent Klaviyo path is templates-only. New action surface (12 new actions): automation-get / automation-create / automation-update / automation-delete / automation-activate / automation-deactivate / automation-steps / automation-step-create / automation-step-update / automation-step-delete / bulk-import-flow. Bulk import takes a JSON manifest under assets/brands/<brand>/sms/flows.json (refused otherwise — same path-traversal safety rail as the Klaviyo session); runs a four-rule TCPA gate on every flow BEFORE any HTTP call; refuses (never auto-fixes) flows that violate. The four rules: (1) every flow's first send_message MUST contain Reply-STOP opt-out language (literal or via {{UNSUB_REPLY}} which token-swaps to 'Reply STOP to unsubscribe'); (2) trigger.type must be on the documented-consent allowlist (subscriber_added_to_list / keyword / checkout / checkout_completed / product_purchased / cart_abandoned / browse_abandoned / back_in_stock — manual-segment triggers refused as no-consent-guarantee); (3) brand must have postscriptTenDLCID configured (US 10DLC mandatory); (4) flow must have ≥1 send_message step. Token swap rewrites generic placeholders ({{FIRST_NAME}}, {{COUPON_CODE}}, {{COUPON_URL}}, {{SHOP_URL}}, {{UNSUB_REPLY}}) to Postscript-native merge tokens before send. Live-state dedup: bulk-import lists existing automations once and refuses to create a duplicate-by-name (idempotent re-imports); pass forceReimport=true to override. Two-phase fan-out: Phase 1 sequential gate (CheckFlowTCPA + dedup, pure CPU), Phase 2 ExecuteBatch('postscript', createRequests) + ExecuteBatch('postscript', activateRequests) — both use the registered postscriptBatchExecute (ParallelFetch concurrency 4). Every outbound api.postscript.io call goes through PreflightCheck + RecordSuccess/RecordRateLimitHit per CLAUDE.md hard rule. New tests: 31 Go tests in postscript_automations_test.go (token swap × 4, TCPA gate × 7, automation CRUD × 9, step CRUD × 4, manifest path-traversal × 3, bulk-import × 4 + parallelism guard); 4 new Node tests in mcp-tools.test.js (description-surface contract + routing-prefix oracle). v1.20.1: Klaviyo email template bulk upload — drop a folder of HTML emails and Merlin uploads them all to Klaviyo with one MCP tool call (klaviyo({action: 'templates-bulk-upload', brand, dir, nameTemplate})). New action surface: templates-list / template-get / template-create / template-update / template-delete / templates-bulk-upload, all routed through klaviyo_templates.go in the Go binary so they ride the existing vault-decrypt path (the user's raw API key is AES-256-GCM-encrypted on disk; only the binary can read it — a Python script that reads klaviyoApiKey from .merlin-config-<brand>.json gets a 401 because the field is the @@VAULT:klaviyoApiKey@@ placeholder, not the raw key). Token swap is opt-in (default true for bulk-upload, false for single template-create): translates {{UNSUB_URL}} → {{ unsubscribe }}, {{ FIRST_NAME }} → {{ first_name|default:'there' }}, {{ EMAIL }} → {{ person.email }}, {{COMPANY_NAME}} / {{COMPANY_ADDRESS}} from brand.md. Bulk-upload validates the directory is inside assets/brands/<brand>/ (no arbitrary FS access), caps each HTML at 5 MB, fans out via ParallelFetch at concurrency 3 (matches klaviyoBatchExecutor's cap, well inside Klaviyo's burst budget), and returns a structured per-file pass/fail report so chat can show 'uploaded 49 of 51, 2 failed: <names>' with no confabulation. Klaviyo Flows themselves remain UI-only — flow construction (trigger, branches, time delays, message slot wiring) is not in the public API as of revision 2024-10-15, and the merlin-social SKILL surfaces this manual step verbatim after every bulk upload so the LLM never tells the user 'I created your flows.' Live incident anchor (2026-04-29, POG): Ryan tried to bulk-import 51 templates for a 27-flow / 51-email setup; the existing klaviyo MCP tool only exposed performance / lists / campaigns; falling back to a Python script hit the 401 above. 25 new Go tests + 4 new MCP tests cover token swap, pagination, oversize rejection, dir traversal rejection, brand-slug validation, partial-success counts, and applyTokens=false byte-preservation. v1.20.0: MCP-direct sidecar — use Merlin's full tool surface from Claude Desktop (or any MCP client: Codex, Cline, Cursor) instead of being tied to the in-app chat. Three additive components, zero regressions to existing flows. (1) Local IPC endpoint inside the desktop app (app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.js) — Unix domain socket on macOS/Linux (mode 0o600), Windows named pipe under default-deny ACL. Newline-delimited JSON protocol, three methods only: ping / tools/list / tools/call. NO vault/get, NO config/read — the endpoint refuses anything else, so a compromised shim cannot exfiltrate keys. Auth via 32-hex token rotated on every desktop-app boot, written to <stateDir>/mcp-shim-token (mode 0o600), constant-time-compared at every dispatch (Hard-Won Security Rule 5). The same wrapped tool array the in-app SDK uses is dispatched here — single registry, two consumers, brand-check + idempotency + preview gate + concurrency slot + redaction all apply uniformly. (2) Stdio MCP shim (app/merlin-mcp-shim.js) — ~400 lines, asar-unpacked so Claude Desktop can spawn it directly with the bundled Node binary. JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout. Active-brand auto-injection: if the call is missing brand AND the renderer's <appRoot>/.merlin-state.json has activeBrand set, the shim injects it before forwarding. Mirrors the in-app [ACTIVE_BRAND] tag flow. Graceful degradation: if the desktop app isn't running, tools/list returns a single placeholder tool whose call surfaces a friendly 'open Merlin and try again' message instead of breaking the connection. (3) Claude Desktop autoconfig (app/claude-desktop-config.js) — atomic merge of {mcpServers: {merlin: {command, args}}} into the user's claude_desktop_config.json. Preserves all other mcpServers entries (Cline, Cursor, etc.) untouched. One-time prompt at first launch with three buttons: Add now / Skip / Don't ask again. Decision persisted to <stateDir>/.mcp-claude-desktop-prompt; 'never' suppresses forever, 'skipped' re-prompts only on a major version bump. Refuses to clobber a corrupt config file. Renderer-driven 'Add to Claude Desktop' button wired via mcp-autoconfig-add IPC; sidecar-status query via mcp-autoconfig-status. SAFETY: this is purely additive — the in-app SDK chat is unchanged, the in-process MCP server still uses the same tool array, no flow that worked in v1.19.5 has changed. Boundary preservation verified: vault stays in Electron, never touches the shim or Claude Desktop process; only tool inputs and redacted outputs cross IPC. Tests: app/mcp-ipc-endpoint.test.js (24 cases) + app/merlin-mcp-shim.test.js (28 cases) + app/claude-desktop-config.test.js (19 cases). | v1.19.5: SDK parity RSI — five fixes landing together to close the felt gap with Claude.ai / Claude Desktop after Ryan called the in-app chat experience 'noticeably horrid' on 2026-04-29 (POG brand, live incidents listed below). Read alongside D:\autoCMO-claude\CLAUDE.md's Engineering Standard section. (A) Mid-turn message queue — silent-drop bug fix + visible badge. messageGenerator's queue drain at app/main.js:2691 lived BEFORE the while(true) await loop, so any message typed during a tool call (resolveNextMessage was null) landed in pendingMessageQueue and was never delivered when the SDK pulled the next message. The drain now runs INSIDE the loop, BEFORE every await — a queued message is delivered on the next turn boundary. Paired UX fix: main.js fires 'message-queued' (depth grows) and 'message-queue-drained' (depth shrinks) IPC events, the renderer's chat-status row now renders a 'queued (N)' badge fed by the pure queueBadgeReducer (app/sdk-parity.js, 9 unit tests). Live incident: Ryan typed '?' and 'helloe?' mid-turn while a tool was hung; both messages disappeared. (B) Mandatory pre-tool grounding — merlin-content/SKILL.md now opens with a five-step MANDATORY pre-tool checklist (brand resolved, product named, references loaded from products/<slug>/references/, brand.md read, pre-tool status emitted) that gates EVERY image/video/voice/refine call. Live incident: agent generated 12 product images for POG without loading references; output was generic, $1+ wasted, 36min wall clock burned. The checklist is the prevention. (C) Confabulation prevention — universal rule in commands/merlin.md: when a tool returns an error, surface it VERBATIM. Never paraphrase into a plausible-sounding root cause without supporting evidence. Banned: invented internals like 'the binary's slug registry' (incident: agent confabulated this when fal returned a plain 404). app/sdk-parity.js exports isRawErrorVerbatim helper (24-char-window heuristic) for future telemetry-side flagging. (D) Pre-tool status discipline — strengthened the rule to require ALL FOUR concrete fields (action, count, context, ETA-or-cost) for any tool >15s OR >$0.05. Vague 'Working on it…' is a regression. Concrete reference example baked in: 'Generating 12 images of POG cherry using 4 references from products/pog-cherry/references/ — ~36min, ~$1.20 fal spend.' app/sdk-parity.js exports formatPreToolStatus that returns null if any required field missing — gives the LLM a programmatic STOP signal. (E) Streaming responsiveness audit — main.js startSession's for-await loop and renderer's textBuffer flush were inspected. accountInfo() is already fire-and-forget (REGRESSION GUARD 2026-04-27); no new sync I/O introduced in v1.19.5. The remaining chunky-feel vs claude.ai is structural — Anthropic SDK's streaming cadence + the SDK subprocess boundary — and not addressable without rewriting the entire SDK invocation. Flagged in PR body, not pretended-fixed. Tests: app/sdk-parity.test.js (26 tests covering reducer state machine, pre-tool status formatter rejection rules, raw-error verbatim heuristic, queue badge copy, pluralizer); existing test suites (oauth-persist 15, bulk-upload 26, captions, paste-drop 28, validate-skills 10) green; routing-corpus extended with 4 new merlin-content utterances covering 'make 12 ads of the cherry product' / 'shoot ad images using the photos I uploaded' / etc. v1.19.4: Three hardenings landing together to prevent the 2026-04-29 trypog.co WebFetch hang from recurring + clean up the misleading Meta-token error chain. (1) Brand-URL routing rule — commands/merlin.md now hard-pushes 'go to my site' / 'rescrape <url>' / 'check products on <url>' intents to mcp__merlin__brand_scrape (which has the 5/15/90s layered timeouts from CLAUDE.md Rule 13). The thin always-loaded router teaches the LLM to never WebFetch the user's own brand URL — built-in WebFetch has no enforced timeout and hung for 5+ minutes on a slow CDN. Universal rule added: WebFetch is a last-resort tool, used only when no MCP tool covers the intent; pre-fetch tells the user 'this can hang, hit Cancel if it stalls.' (2) Cancel button on the chat-status row — fades in at 30s of stuck status, calls the new abort-active-query IPC which interrupt()s the active SDK turn, drains the pending message queue, clears pending tool approvals, and broadcasts query-aborted so the renderer can clear the spinner + show a 'Cancelled' toast. Re-uses the same activeQuery.interrupt() machinery the brand-switch handler has used since v1.18.x. Means future hung tools are recoverable in one click instead of force-quitting Merlin. (3) Meta empty-token error message hardening — five fatal() sites in meta.go (2331, 2841, 3587, 3782, 4033) all said 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again,' which is exactly what the user had JUST done via the right-click 'Use my API key' modal in the trust-loss incident. New tokenMissingDiag helper (token_required.go) builds context-aware messages: brand-context → 'token saved correctly but invisible to this code path, restart Merlin and retry'; no-brand-context → 'connect Meta via the Magic panel tile, or paste a long-lived token via right-click "Use my API key".' 4 unit tests pin both cases and assert the pre-fix misleading hint can never reappear. Audit list for extending to tiktok/google/linkedin/amazon kept inline in token_required.go for follow-up. v1.19.3: HOTFIX — Meta manual-token modal: pasting an access token via right-click → 'Use my API key' on a brand-scoped tile (e.g. POG) silently failed with 'metaAccessToken required — paste your token and run this again', even though the token was correctly persisted to the brand vault. Root cause: ipcMain.handle('discover-meta-ids') in app/main.js spawned the Go binary with --config <global-config> only — the binary's loadConfig does NOT auto-merge .merlin-config-<brand>.json from disk, so the @@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@ placeholder (which the renderer wrote to the brand config file) never made it into the binary's Config struct, applyBrandVault had nothing to resolve, and cfg.MetaAccessToken stayed empty. Dashboard refresh works because it builds a strict brand-merged config in a temp file via buildStrictBrandConfig and passes THAT as --config (line ~6253). discoverMetaIds now mirrors the dashboard pattern exactly — temp config, brand-merged, deleted on completion (no decrypted credentials left on disk). REGRESSION GUARD comment added at the fix site documenting the live incident (brand=pog, 2026-04-28, screenshot in chat). v1.19.2: TWO landed-together features — (A) window-level paste/drop into the chat composer (replaces the v1.19.1 bulk-upload tile), and (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap from BFF (worker secret = single source of truth). | (A) Bulk-upload UX refactor — kill the tile, embrace window-level paste/drop. The dedicated 'Bulk upload' Magic-panel tile (shipped in v1.19.1) is retired. Modern apps (Linear, Slack, ChatGPT desktop, Claude.ai, Cursor) all handle file input as window-level paste/drop into the composer; the LLM sees attachments alongside the user's typed context and decides routing — much smarter than a rigid filename Jaro-Winkler matcher running on every drop. The IPC backend stays shipped, refactored into a reusable helper that's exposed to the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool — the LLM calls it on demand when batch routing makes sense. | (B) OAuth client_id bootstrap — every provider's clientID now flows from the BFF (Cloudflare Worker secret <FOO>_CLIENT_ID) through the exchange response into cfg.OAuthFoo.ClientID. Hardcoded values inside oauth.go's getFooOAuth factories become first-launch fallbacks only; the canonical value lives in one place — the Cloudflare secret. Closes the LinkedIn debug-loop class of incidents. New CLAUDE.md Hard-Won Security Rule 17 documents the four required call sites for adding a new OAuth provider.
whatsNew: Fix: every Merlin tool is back. v1.21.0's SDK upgrade had a bug that quietly hid all mcp__merlin__* tools after your first message in any brand — "can you push this ad" / "show me the dashboard" / "audit my pixel" suddenly returned "I don't see that tool." Fixed by skipping the SDK's session-resume call until Anthropic ships their fix. Trade-off you might notice: Claude no longer remembers context between your turns at the SDK layer (each message starts fresh from the SDK's perspective), but the chat history you see in Merlin is unchanged and tools work — which is the whole point.

New: Google Analytics 4 is now wired in end-to-end. Ask Merlin "how is organic traffic", "what's converting in GA", "first-touch vs last-touch by channel", "is my GA4 set up right", and Merlin pulls the data directly from your GA4 property. First connect — or after this update — Google's consent screen will show one new permission (Analytics read+write); approve once and you're done.

New: Merlin can also SET UP your GA4 measurement plan, not just read it. "Set up the standard ecommerce events in GA4" runs the new attach-shopify-events action — idempotent one-click setup that marks purchase / add_to_cart / begin_checkout / view_item / view_item_list / search / sign_up / generate_lead as GA4 key events, replacing 30 minutes of click-through-Admin with one tool call. Other write actions: create-key-event, archive-key-event, create-custom-dimension, create-custom-metric, create-audience (for funnel/cohort analysis), update-property-settings (timezone / currency / industry category). Every write asks for approval first; the binary refuses to mutate your property without explicit user click.

New: ask Merlin to audit your Meta account end-to-end. "Audit my retargeting cascade" walks every active ad set and flags the classic leak — including site visitors but forgetting to exclude past purchasers, so you pay to retarget your own buyers. "Is my pixel healthy?" runs match-rate diagnostics + 7-day event coverage and tells you exactly what to fix (low match rate usually means missing em/ph/fbp/fbc params; CAPI typically lifts to 70%+). "Are my ad sets capped?" lists every uncapped ad set as a fatigue risk. "Is my catalog healthy?" returns review-status counts + top disapproval reasons. All read-only — no writes, no spend, safe to call freely.

New: meta_audit also surfaces the lower-frequency utility actions — list every custom audience (sorted newest-first with approximate count + operation/delivery status), read the full targeting rule for one audience, list every custom conversion (with last-fired time so you can spot dormant ones). Replaces a trip to Ads Manager with one natural-language question.

Audit calls are wired into Merlin's rate-limit preflight, so even big walks (e.g. 200 ad sets in the cascade audit) can't stampede past Meta's per-minute budget. No risk of triggering a 4-hour Meta rate-limit ban from running diagnostics.

Fixed: the mobile QR experience no longer drops after ~5 minutes of inactivity. Mobile carrier NAT idle timeouts were silently killing the WebSocket TCP connection in the background, so when you came back to your phone, your next message appeared to send but actually disconnected. Both the desktop side and your phone now exchange a tiny keepalive every 25 seconds to keep the connection fresh — well below the 30-second floor of every common carrier NAT, and the relay server handles it for free without burning your session's rate-limit budget. If a connection does die for any reason, the keepalive watchdog detects it within 60 seconds and reconnects automatically — versus the old behavior of waiting 2-10 minutes for the OS to notice.

Fixed: Mac DMG builds now have a fallback download mirror for ffmpeg, so a transient outage at the canonical upstream (evermeet.cx) doesn't block a release. Live incident: v1.21.0 first attempt failed twice in 30 minutes because evermeet.cx was unreachable from GitHub Actions Mac runners; the SHA-identical mirror at deolaha.ca was up the entire time but our build script only knew about the public face. Both URLs now in release.yml; SHA verification runs unmodified on either source.

Fixed: Klaviyo flow-create now sends the correct request body. v1.20.7 inferred the shape from prose; the actual OpenAPI spec requires temporary_id on every action, entry_action_id pointing to the first action, links between actions, and trigger.type='list' (not 'list_added'). Live import now succeeds against revision 2026-04-15.

Klaviyo Flows are now fully programmatic. Drop a JSON manifest of email automations under assets/brands/<brand>/email/flows.json and Merlin creates them all via the Klaviyo Flows API — same shape as the v1.20.2 Postscript bulk-flow-import. Reference templates by their template_id from a prior templates-bulk-upload pass. (Correction: yesterday's v1.20.1 release notes claimed Klaviyo Flows were UI-only; that was wrong — the API exists, we just hadn't wrapped it.)

CAN-SPAM gate refuses any flow missing an unsubscribe link, physical mailing address, or with a trigger type outside the documented-consent allowlist (list_added, segment_added, profile_subscribed_marketing, ecommerce_placed_order, ecommerce_started_checkout, viewed_product, custom_event). Failures surface verbatim — never auto-fixed; you see exactly which flow blocked and which rule fired.

Fixed: Claude Desktop / Claude Code MCP server no longer disconnects silently when Merlin restarts. Pre-fix, after every Merlin /update or app restart, the IPC socket dropped and the shim process exited cleanly (Claude Desktop saw 'Server disconnected' with no error) — you had to manually restart Claude Desktop to get tools back. The shim now keeps its event loop alive across socket drops and reconnects on the next tool call automatically.

Fixed: every shim error path now writes a labeled line to stderr (which Claude Desktop captures into its log). Previous 'Server disconnected' events surfaced with no diagnostic; future failures come with a cause inline (parse error, IPC connect failure, dispatch exception, uncaught rejection, etc.).

Fixed: 'Add to Claude Code' now actually enables the server in your project. Previously the server was registered in .mcp.json but never made it into .claude.json's enabledMcpjsonServers allowlist, so Claude Code refused to load it for security. The auto-config now writes both files atomically — pick which project to enable Merlin in, and Claude Code can use Merlin's tools immediately.

New: Magic panel sidecar status now distinguishes Claude Desktop and Claude Code. Each project you've connected for Claude Code is listed individually with a live/stale dot so you can re-enable any project where the registration drifted (a teammate edited .mcp.json by hand, ~/.claude.json got reset, etc.).

Fixed: Merlin works in Claude Code now. The MCP sidecar's tools/list response was emitting tool inputSchemas without an explicit `type: "object"` at the top level — Claude Desktop tolerated this, Claude Code strictly rejected every tool ("inputSchema.type missing or not object"). Now every emitted schema is normalized to JSON Schema 7's MCP contract before it crosses the wire: type:"object" + properties + additionalProperties guaranteed on every tool, drift-proof against future zod / zod-to-json-schema version changes.

Fixed: Klaviyo bulk-template-upload now reliably finishes large batches (27+ templates) without rate-limit failures. Sequential pacing + 6s spacing + automatic retry on 429 means the import takes ~3 min instead of ~9s, but actually completes — versus the prior path where ~10 templates would land then ~8 would 429 and get marked permanently failed.

Postscript SMS flow import — drop a JSON manifest of automations (triggers + steps + messages) under assets/brands/<brand>/sms/ and Merlin creates them all via API. Unlike Klaviyo Flows (UI-only), Postscript Automations are fully scriptable end-to-end, so the morning SMS setup goes from 30 min of point-and-click to one tool call. TCPA gates every import: STOP opt-out language required in every flow's first message, trigger.type must be on the opt-in allowlist, A2P 10DLC brand verified, and quiet-hours respected. Failures REFUSE (no auto-fix) so you see exactly which flow blocked and why.

New: use Merlin from Claude Desktop. The tile-driven setup (brand picker, OAuth, daemons) stays in Merlin; the chat happens wherever you want — Claude Desktop, Codex, Cline, Cursor, any MCP client. On first launch (and after major upgrades), Merlin offers a one-click 'Add to Claude Desktop' that atomically merges itself into claude_desktop_config.json without touching your other MCP servers. Use it as a polished sidecar until the in-app chat catches up.

Security: your API keys never leave Merlin's encrypted vault. Claude Desktop only sees tool inputs and outputs — exactly the same boundary the in-app chat respects. The local IPC endpoint refuses any call outside ping / tools/list / tools/call, so a compromised MCP client can only invoke Merlin tools, never read your tokens.

Active brand syncs automatically. Pick the brand in Merlin's Magic panel; Claude Desktop tools pick it up without you having to type 'brand: pog' on every call. Override is still respected — explicit brand args are never overwritten.
Auth token rotates on every Merlin restart. A stale Claude Desktop tab from a previous session safely refuses old credentials and reconnects on the next call.

Type while Merlin is thinking — your message queues and a 'queued (1)' badge appears next to the spinner so you know it landed. Previously messages typed during a tool call disappeared into a void; the queue existed but the agent never picked them up after the tool finished. Now the next turn drains everything you typed in order.

Merlin announces what it's about to do before any tool that takes >15s or spends real money — including the brand, product, and reference images it's using. Catch a wrong context BEFORE 30+ minutes of generation spend, not after. Vague 'working on it…' is gone.

Image and video generation now requires a real product + reference photos. Merlin will STOP and ask 'which product?' if the brand has multiple products and you didn't name one — instead of silently generating something generic. Closes the live incident where 12 ads got generated without any of the brand's actual references.

Errors stay raw — Merlin no longer guesses at root causes when a tool fails. If it doesn't know what went wrong, it says so and asks what to try next. Banned: invented internals like 'the binary's slug registry doesn't recognize this' when no such structure exists.
Cancel button still escapes hung tool calls, and now correctly clears the queue badge too — clicking Cancel with messages queued shows them cleared so you know the abort worked end-to-end.

Klaviyo template bulk upload — drop a folder of email HTMLs and Merlin uploads them all to Klaviyo with one command, applying token translations ({{UNSUB_URL}} → Klaviyo Django tags) and a configurable naming template. Note: Klaviyo Flows themselves still need to be wired in Klaviyo's UI — flow construction isn't in the public API.

Fixed: any fal.ai model under any namespace now works — pass openai/gpt-image-2/edit, black-forest-labs/flux-pro, or any future-lab/model/v1 and Merlin sends it through unchanged. Previously the binary auto-prepended fal-ai/ to every slash-containing slug, corrupting non-fal-ai/ namespaces into 404s. Live incident: openai/gpt-image-2/edit was unreachable.

New: Cancel button on the chat status row. When a tool call hangs (slow website fetch, third-party API stall), a Cancel button fades in next to the spinner after 30s — one click stops the turn. Previously the only escape was force-quitting Merlin.

Fixed: 'go to my site and check products' / 'rescrape <url>' now reliably routes to the brand-scrape tool (which has hardened 5/15/90s timeouts) instead of the built-in WebFetch (which has no timeout). Live incident: trypog.co rescrape hung the chat for 5+ minutes.

Fixed: when Merlin can't find a Meta access token, the error now says exactly what to do — 'restart Merlin and retry' for brand-scoped tiles, or 'connect Meta via the Magic panel' for global. Previously the same five fatal sites all said 'paste your token and run this again' — which loops the user into the same failure when the actual bug was upstream.

Fixed: pasting a Meta access token via the right-click → 'Use my API key' modal on a brand-scoped tile now works end-to-end. Previously the discover step failed with 'metaAccessToken required' even though the token was correctly saved — the binary spawn passed only the global config, missing the brand's vault placeholder. Now uses the same brand-merged temp config the dashboard uses.

Paste screenshots or drag-drop files anywhere in the window — they attach to your next message as chips above the composer and Merlin decides what to do with them (Read the image, file it with a product, embed in an ad brief, whatever the context calls for).

Removed the dedicated Bulk upload tile from the Magic panel — the LLM is smarter about routing than a rigid filename matcher. The Go Jaro-Winkler matcher stays shipped, exposed as the new mcp__merlin__bulk_upload tool that Merlin calls on demand when batch-filing 5+ files into product folders is clearly the right move.

Hardened (paste/drop chip flow): pasted clipboard images materialize to assets/brands/<brand>/inbox/ with a SHA-prefixed filename that contains zero user-controlled bytes (eliminates injection); 25 MB cap on pasted blobs (clipboard is RAM); MIME allowlist enforced server-side; counter-based dragenter/dragleave prevents the flicker that single-boolean overlays produce when the cursor crosses child elements.

Hardened: every OAuth provider's client_id now flows from the Cloudflare Worker secret as the single source of truth. The binary's hardcoded values are first-launch fallbacks only — the canonical value lives in one place, never two. Closes a class of debug loops where 'is the binary's hardcoded ID the same as the secret?' couldn't be answered (Wrangler doesn't reveal secret values).

Hardened: 35 new Go tests (oauth_bootstrap_test.go) source-grep both the binary and worker.js to fail CI on any drift between the hardcoded fallbacks. Add a new OAuth provider in 4 places? CI checks all 4. Miss one? Test fails before merge.
